includedInDataCatalog.name: Z-Beam Contamination Removal Database
includedInDataCatalog.description: Comprehensive database of contamination removal parameters, safety data, and laser cleaning specifications

contaminant.name: Gold Electroplating
contaminant.description: Gold-plating contamination hits when thin gold layers flake off or transfer during handling of electronics and jewelry, often forming through wear on base metals like copper or nickel. It stands out with its shiny, stubborn metallic film that clings tighter than typical oxides or grime, showing up brighter and harder to scrape without scratching. On brass, it bonds deep and resists solvents, while on copper it loosens faster but still demands care to avoid base damage—laser cleaning nails this by zapping the gold precisely, clearing it clean without harming the substrate underneath.

fireExplosionRisk.description: Minimal fire risk with standard precautions and adequate ventilation
fireExplosionRisk.mitigation: Standard fire safety precautions, extinguisher available within 15m

toxicGasRisk.description: Gold nanoparticles and Cyanide compounds generation - multiple toxic compounds
toxicGasRisk.mitigation: Half-face or full-face respirator with organic vapor/particulate cartridges, adequate ventilation

visibilityHazard.description: Light haze (20-40% reduction), minimal impact on sight lines

ventilationRequirements.rationale: Standard industrial ventilation (12 ACH) for particulate control
